strategy: Group SMART attributes into 4 physically meaningful health categories

carriers.Errors: SMART 1 (Read Error) / 1e6 + SMART 7 (Seek Error) / 1e6 + SMART 199 (UDMA CRC)

decompositions.A: Per-drive: each drive is one observation (N=5000 subsampled, D=4)
decompositions.B: Per-model: mean SMART scores per model (N=52, D=4)

data_quality_findings.fleet_composition: Mixed HDD/SSD fleet — only 15.2% are HDD with standard SMART
data_quality_findings.reallocated_sectors: 90.8% of drives show zero — fleet is mostly healthy
data_quality_findings.failure_rate: 0.014% daily — extremely low, making failure detection a rare-event problem
data_quality_findings.recommendation: Separate HDD and SSD populations before decomposition. Use 4-carrier [Mechanical, Thermal, Age, Errors] not crude 3-carrier [Healthy, Warning, Failed].
